text:
Market today: Mood is cautiously constructive - inflation traders on Kalshi see a peak in May with energy dragging June lower, which is a real tailwind for rate-sensitive tech and chips. The CoreWeave/Nebius selloff on Meta cloud competition is a sector-specific headwind worth watching for anything adjacent to AI infrastructure.

Your holdings: SOXL (-11%, leveraged 3x chips) is the one screaming for attention - it's still above your $200 stop but leveraged decay eats you alive on margin; any chip wobble accelerates the pain. SPAL (-10.78%, brand-new very volatile leveraged IPO) is already within cents of its $21.38 stop - margin amplifies this one hard. PTNM (-15.25%) is a tiny spec bleeding; stop at $9.50 is close.

Watch: (1) Whether the chip complex (SMH, SOXL) gets a bid from the inflation/rate narrative or rolls over on AI-cloud competition fears. (2) SPAL's $21.38 stop - it's right there.

Bottom line: Your leveraged losers on margin are the only fire to put out today - everything else can wait.
plays: SPAL: Stop is $21.38 and price is $22.44 - it's basically there. No reason to fight it; let the stop work or cut manually now if you're uncomfortable on margin.
SOXL: -11% and leveraged 3x on margin is a dangerous combo. Consider trimming to reduce margin exposure; hard stop $200 is your line in the sand - don't move it lower.
No new buys today - chip setups (AMAT, MU, KLAC) are flagged premium/chasing by the scanner and the CoreWeave/Meta cloud headline adds sector noise. Hold and watch.
